candidates.label: Minimalistic status update
candidates.description: Short single-word or very brief status updates.
candidates.boundary_note: A longer post such as 'hmmmmm. I thought I'd be Stanley' shows the same brief opening but with additional commentary.

candidates.label: Quotative emphasis with quotation marks
candidates.description: Use of quotation marks to emphasize random words or phrases for comedic effect.

citations.text: yummy "meat" pressed into the shape of "ribs?"
finds it humorous that people are using the words "credibility" and "American Idol" in the same sentence today.
Either this app is very bad, or my "Robot Name" is unpronounceable in English.
RIP Billy Mays, I hope the 'Sham-Wow' guy is OK.

candidates.boundary_note: Posts that contain only apostrophes, such as 'B'ham', do not use quotation marks for emphasis.

candidates.label: URL sharing in status
candidates.description: Embedding external URLs within status updates.

citations.text: Can't they at least spell check the headline? http://www.thestate.com/breaking/story/916228.html

candidates.boundary_note: A status such as 'I just found out I have jury duty...' contains no URL, illustrating that not all personal updates share links.
